Divinity's Reach is divided into neighborhoods and residences. Of all the city's twisting streets, this is the place that you call home, where familiar faces smile to see you, and your friends await to buy you a drink, or listen to your tales.

Divinity's Reach is the greatest city in the human nation of Kryta. It is laid out like a giant wheel. Its upper city contains Queen Jennah's royal palace and the Chamber of Ministers. Six high roads, each dedicated to a god, divide the lower city into districts.

Divinity's Reach is the greatest city of Kryta, the human nation. It is laid out like a giant wheel, and its central plaza contains Queen Jennah's royal palace and the Chamber of Ministers. Six high roads, each dedicated to a god, divide the lower city into districts.
Division is repeatable and increases placement on the legendary leaderboard.

Djinn runecrafters have long used these vessels to cleanse magical artifacts and immerse them in desired energies. Crafters traditionally dwell within them during the process.    The interior crystals are often mistaken for Branding by modern observers, but they are simply clusters of cultivated amethysts.
Do a quick roll to increase your speed.
Do an evasive attack over your target, striking nearby foes three times. During this attack, you reflect projectiles.
Do an evasive dodge roll, negating damage, in the direction your character is moving (backward if stationary).
Do an evasive roll around your target, striking them and poisoning them.
Do as the Mad King commands.
Do holograms feel pain? I think that one did.

Do I relish the thought of wading each day through a mountain of titan innards and crumbling mursaat architecture, all at heavy risk to my life? No. But consider the prize—an active Mists gate! And one which has lain undisturbed for centuries at that! What alien objects might have tumbled their way across space and time to finally end up in our little corner of the Mists? Consider the insight offered by these priceless artifacts—some, perhaps, hailing from unknown worlds!    For posterity, I shall begin a catalogue of each and every object found by our dig site team, starting in the area closest to the gate.    —Gosta, Lead of Metaphysical Studies     **OBJECT 1824.A.102**      *Name*: Ornate Chalice  *Description*: Silver, moderate loss of surface. Evidence of past inlays around the stem. Traces of dark residue on interior. Bad feeling while holding it.  Notes: Bag this one to avoid future dishware mix-ups.    **OBJECT 1824.A.511**    *Name*: Etched Stone Piece  *Description*: Dark in color, palm sized. Single surviving shard; plano-convex, likely once part of globular whole. Groove patterns seem deliberate. Ritual object?  Notes: Made hands tingle a little. Exciting!    **OBJECTS 1824.B.422-435**  *Name*: Mirror(?) Pieces  *Description*: Material unknown (not of mursaat origin). Subtriangular shape and light refraction suggest surviving pieces of mirror or a mirrorlike object. Definitely magical. Currently attempting reconstruction.   Notes: What's the worst that could happen?

Dodge to avoid danger by pressing the %str1% key or by double-tapping a movement key. While dodging, you're temporarily invulnerable. The yellow endurance bar above your health drains with each dodge, but it will recover over time.
Dodge tutorials are in Plains of Ashford, Wayfarer Foothills, Queensdale, Caledon Forest, and Metrica Province.
Dodging allows you to evade incoming attacks by spending endurance to roll out of danger, making you temporarily invulnerable.
Dodging has a chance to heal allies.

Don't trust that Canthan lady. Not one bit.Mostly keeps to herself. When she's not talking with Ankka, she sticks close to camp or wanders off on her own. She watches us but doesn't have a whole lot to say.Tries to come across as friendly, but you get a little peek of that grimace from time to time. Don't like it. Called Faas "repugnant" when he stepped on her shoe.Spends a lot of time staring at that journal. Seems like she writes down every thought or song that pops into her head. Gonna see if I can swipe a few pages...get them to the boss.
